place: South Seaville CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: South Seaville’s railroad station, built in 1897, was part of the Ocean City Railroad line that later joined the Pennsylvania‑Reading Seashore Lines.
South Seaville’s historic Methodist Camp Meeting Grounds feature Victorian cottages that have hosted gatherings since the 1800s.
The town’s name, South Seaville, reflects its position just south of the larger Seaville community, a detail noted in local museum records.
A 1910 photograph in the Dennis Township Museum shows the original South Seaville School, a key community hub in the early 1900s.
South Seaville’s historic train depot once served as a factory and office for Collins Lumber, illustrating the town’s early industrial roots.
